title: Massachusetts Senate passes bill targeting addictive social media features for kids
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The Massachusetts Senate passed a bill targeting addictive social media features for minors, with 38 members voting in favor.
The bill proposes disabling autoplay, infinite scroll, and push notifications for minors between 10 p.m. and 6 a.m.
Social media companies would need to file annual reports on how they target minors under the new legislation.

title: Police seize dog who severely injured smaller pup in Chelsea attack caught on video
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Chelsea police seized a dog that attacked a smaller pup on Chestnut Street, seriously injuring it.
The larger dog, unlicensed, is in quarantine for 10 days as officials consider potential law violations.
The injured smaller dog, a service animal named Snoopy Snoop Dog, requires leg amputation after the attack.

title: Mac Jones opens up about his tumultuous time with the Patriots
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Mac Jones' tenure with the Patriots ended with a trade to Jacksonville for a sixth-round pick.
Jones cited offensive struggles after Josh McDaniels' departure, leading to confusion with play-calling duties.
Jones found his trade to Jacksonville refreshing, marking a new chapter in his career.

title: France vs. Morocco live coverage: ‘Boston Stadium’ hosts high-profile World Cup quarterfinal matchup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France and Morocco face off in a World Cup quarterfinal at Gillette Stadium, the final 2026 World Cup match in Foxborough.
Both teams previously played in Foxborough: Morocco defeated Scotland, France defeated Norway in earlier World Cup games.
Moroccan forward Ismael Saibari is ruled out due to injury; temperatures are nearing 90 degrees during the match.

title: ‘He hit me’: Pregame France vs. Morocco press conference briefly halted after journalist altercation
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: A pregame press conference for the France-Morocco World Cup match was disrupted by a journalist altercation at Gillette Stadium.
FIFA officials intervened as a Moroccan player, Brahim Díaz, witnessed two reporters arguing, reportedly after one was hit.
The incident did not relate to the upcoming match, and French coach Didier Deschamps faced media questions separately.

title: After long search, scientists get amazing great white shark footage off R.I. coast
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Scientists captured footage of an eight-foot great white shark near a whale carcass southeast of Block Island in June.
Jon Dodd and Sarah Callan located the dead humpback whale after covering over 50 square miles of ocean.
The shark, believed to be a juvenile based on size, was in good health and showed no signs of tagging.

title: Iconic Christmas Tree Shops signs are headed to auction
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Two 27-foot Christmas Tree Shops signs from the former Hyannis store are being auctioned starting at $1,000.
Proceeds from the auction will benefit Habitat for Humanity of Cape Cod, which supports affordable housing projects.
The Hyannis location is being transformed into a Best Buy, set to open on July 31, after CTS's 2023 bankruptcy.

title: A moment of appreciation for dominant Red Sox ace Sonny Gray
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Sonny Gray of the Boston Red Sox is 10-1 with a 2.61 ERA as of Thursday.
Despite his strong performance, Gray was not included in the All-Star team, surprising many, including himself.
Several teams, including the Atlanta Braves, are reportedly interested in acquiring Gray, but the Red Sox might keep him.

title: Lindsay Clancy’s strangulation charges dropped ahead of murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Lindsay Clancy's strangulation charges were dropped by Plymouth County prosecutors as they prepare for her murder trial.
Clancy faces first-degree murder charges for allegedly killing her children in Duxbury, Massachusetts in January 2023.
Her attorney, Kevin Reddington, plans to argue an insanity defense highlighting Clancy's mental health struggles.

title: A modern 3-bedroom condo in Newton — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: A modern 3-bedroom condo in Newton Highlands offers 2,100 square feet with 2 full and 1 half baths.
The condo, built in 2026, has an estimated tax of $15,455 and a monthly fee of $308.
The listing price options for the condo are $1.595 million, $1.395 million, and $1.195 million.
